title: Spinach and Garlic Rice
summary: This bright green rice is such an easy way to get your veg in. You won't find a better healthy garlic rice recipe about.

method.description: Wash the rice two or three times until the water runs clear, then drain.
Add the spinach, water, garlic, and butter to a blender. Blend until smooth and vividly green.
Add the rice to a medium-sized saucepan and pour over the blended spinach mixture.
Bring to a boil, then cover with a lid and reduce the heat to as low as possible. Cook for 12 mins.
Turn off the heat and let the rice sit for 10 mins.
Remove the lid and fluff up the rice with a fork. Some spinach may settle on top – just mix it back through the rice.
Cover with the lid again and let sit for a further 5 mins. Season to taste with salt and black pepper before serving.

tips: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.
To freeze, portion into containers and freeze for up to 3 months. Defrost in the fridge overnight and reheat in a pan or microwave.
Works well as a meal prep base—pair with grilled meats, roasted vegetables, or tofu.
Swap jasmine rice for basmati or long-grain rice.
Use olive oil instead of butter for a dairy-free option.
Add extra greens like kale or parsley for a more nutrient-dense variation.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
